EigenInferenceEnclave.claim: Package structure defines static library and CLI executable products targeting macOS 13+
SecureEnclaveIdentity class manages hardware-bound P-256 keys with creation and loading constructors
Signing operation returns DER-encoded ECDSA signature using Secure Enclave hardware
AttestationBlob struct contains comprehensive hardware and security state fields in alphabetical order
JSON encoding uses .sortedKeys for deterministic output and signs the encoded blob
FFI bridge creates Secure Enclave identities and returns opaque pointers using Unmanaged.passRetained
FFI signing function takes raw bytes and returns base64-encoded DER signature
CLI tool provides attest and info commands with optional encryption key and binary hash parameters
System info helpers use system_profiler and sysctl to collect hardware identification
Security checks include SIP status via csrutil command execution
Tests verify JSON serialization produces sorted keys in alphabetical order for deterministic signing
FFI bridge tests validate C interface for identity creation, key export, and memory management

EigenInferenceEnclaveCLI.claim: CLI provides attestation and info commands with ephemeral key model
Usage function provides user-facing command documentation
cmdAttest function handles attestation generation with hardware checks
cmdInfo function displays Secure Enclave availability and key information
Command-line argument parsing with manual implementation
Command dispatch logic with error handling and exit codes
Secure Enclave availability check before operations
JSON output formatting with sorted keys and ISO8601 dates
Info command output structure with security status
Attest command with optional encryption-key and binary-hash parameters
TLS bridge functionality removed due to Apple security restrictions
CLI target depends on EigenInferenceEnclave library

analytics.claim: The main application orchestrates service initialization with graceful shutdown
HTTP API handler implements three main endpoints with CORS support
Earnings leaderboard endpoint with query parameter validation
Leaderboard service contains core business logic for data aggregation
Store interface provides data access abstraction
Memory store implementation with mock data for development
Pseudonym generator uses HMAC-SHA256 for deterministic aliases
Configuration management with environment variables and validation
External dependencies including pgx PostgreSQL driver
Dual backend support with memory and PostgreSQL options
PostgreSQL store queries providers and provider_earnings tables
Service is designed to sit beside coordinator as standalone analytics API
CORS middleware supports configurable origin allowlist
Leaderboard response structure with earnings, rankings, and metadata

coordinator.claim: The coordinator runs as the central routing and trust layer in the Darkbloom network, accepting provider WebSocket connections and routing OpenAI-compatible HTTP requests
Main function initializes core components including structured logging, database connections, provider registry, and configuration from environment variables
Server struct contains registry, store, billing service, rate limiters, and comprehensive middleware for authentication and observability
Routes function defines comprehensive REST API including consumer inference endpoints, provider WebSocket, billing, and admin interfaces
Registry manages provider fleet with hardware capabilities, trust levels, and round-robin routing among idle providers serving requested models
Consumer API implements OpenAI-compatible endpoints with streaming support, provider failover, and comprehensive timeout handling
Billing service provides unified payment processing via Stripe with support for deposits, Connect Express payouts, and referral rewards
Store interface defines comprehensive persistence layer for API keys, usage tracking, balance ledgers, provider records, and billing sessions
Protocol package defines WebSocket message types for coordinator-provider communication including registration, heartbeat, and inference flows
Payment ledger implements double-entry bookkeeping with micro-USD precision for consumer charges and provider payouts including platform fees
Provider WebSocket handler manages the complete provider lifecycle from registration through attestation verification and inference serving
Telemetry system ingests operational events with field allowlisting and rate limiting, forwarding to Datadog for monitoring
External dependencies include WebSocket library, PostgreSQL driver, cryptographic libraries, JWT validation, UUID generation, rate limiting, and Datadog integration
Datadog integration provides APM tracing, DogStatsD metrics, and Logs API forwarding for comprehensive observability
Billing configuration supports Stripe Checkout, Connect Express, referral system, and mock mode for development
Privy authentication integration handles JWT validation and device authorization flows for consumer accounts
HTTP server configuration includes proper timeouts for streaming inference with graceful shutdown handling
Consumer API implements comprehensive timeout handling with 10-minute inference timeout and first chunk timeout for provider failover
Provider trust levels range from none (no attestation) through self-signed to hardware (MDA + SE key verification)
Model catalog seeding function ensures consistent model availability across coordinator restarts with automatic cleanup of retired models

darkbloom.claim: Darkbloom is designed as an EigenInference provider agent for Apple Silicon Macs with comprehensive architecture documentation
The CLI interface implements 19 subcommands for comprehensive provider management
Coordinator client manages WebSocket connections with automatic reconnection and handles inference request routing
Backend manager implements health monitoring and automatic restart for inference engines
In-process engine provides Phase 3 security by running inference inside the hardened Rust process via PyO3
Security module implements PT_DENY_ATTACH, SIP verification, and memory wiping for runtime protection
Hardware detection module identifies Apple Silicon capabilities including chip family, memory, and GPU cores
Crypto engine implements ephemeral X25519 keypairs for end-to-end encryption with memory protection
Model discovery scans HuggingFace cache and computes SHA-256 fingerprints for weight verification
Telemetry system implements async batching with disk overflow and structured event collection
HTTP server provides legacy local-only proxy with OpenAI-compatible endpoints quarantined for debug use
WebSocket protocol defines comprehensive message types for provider-coordinator communication
Complete dependency specification with version constraints and feature flags
Service management integrates with macOS launchd for user-controlled provider lifecycle
Build script configures coordinator URLs and links Swift Secure Enclave library for compilation

decrypt-test.claim: The component is a minimal NaCl Box decryptor for cross-language compatibility testing
The CLI validates exactly three command-line arguments with specific error handling
Base64 decoding is performed on all three arguments with comprehensive error handling
Key validation ensures both public and private keys are exactly 32 bytes
Ciphertext validation requires minimum 24 bytes for the nonce
SalsaBox is created using X25519 public and private keys for NaCl compatibility
24-byte nonce is extracted from the ciphertext prefix
Decryption uses the SalsaBox with extracted nonce and remaining ciphertext
Output is converted to UTF-8 and printed to stdout
Dependencies include crypto_box 0.9 for NaCl cryptography and base64 0.22 for encoding
Imports base64 standard engine and crypto_box types for key handling and AEAD operations
CLI usage pattern takes three Base64-encoded arguments for decryption testing

verify-attestation.claim: The component reads a fixed attestation file and delegates verification to the coordinator's attestation package, outputting human-readable results with appropriate exit codes
File read errors are handled with stderr output and exit code 1
JSON parsing uses attestation.VerifyJSON() and handles errors with exit code 1
Hardware information is extracted and displayed including chip name and hardware model
Verification results are output with success message for valid attestations or failure message with exit code 1 for invalid ones
The component depends on the coordinator's attestation package for core verification functionality
The component demonstrates cross-language verification between Swift Secure Enclave and Go coordinator implementations
VerifyJSON function parses attestation JSON and delegates to core verification logic
The Verify function performs comprehensive P-256 ECDSA signature validation and security requirement checks
VerificationResult structure contains hardware info, security status, and validation outcomes used by the CLI output

web.claim: Runtime dependencies include Next.js, React, Zustand, TweetNaCl, and Privy for core functionality
Root layout provides authentication, theme, and analytics providers in nested context structure
Chat page component manages streaming AI conversations with authentication and model loading
Zustand store manages chat state with persistence and provides actions for message management
StreamChat function handles optional encryption, request sealing, and coordinator communication
Encryption system uses X25519 NaCl Box with ephemeral keypairs for forward secrecy
Authentication hook handles Privy integration with automatic API key provisioning
Chat API route proxies requests to coordinator with encryption and streaming support
Telemetry system batches events with buffering and automatic retry on network failures
AppShell provides main layout with sidebar navigation and toast notifications
HandleSend function manages chat flow including message creation and streaming API calls
Think block detection supports multiple model formats for reasoning display
Authentication flow handles key migration and automatic provisioning on login
Coordinator key fetching includes caching with TTL and URL-based cache invalidation
Provider dashboard fetches real-time machine status with 15-second refresh intervals
Privy provider configuration uses email-only login with dark theme customization
Google Analytics integration includes consent management and GDPR compliance
Certificate verification uses ASN1.js and PKI.js for hardware attestation validation
Next.js configuration ignores build errors due to viem dependency tree TypeScript issues
API routes pass through attestation and trust headers from coordinator responses
Trust metadata extraction includes hardware attestation details and Secure Enclave verification
